description: Implement all backend API routes and the complete frontend UI including pages for runs list, new run form, run detail with logs, dashboard viewer, and admin prompts management. Includes dark-themed layout with navigation, StatusBadge, RunCard, and other UI components.
steps: Create API routes: src/app/api/runs/route.ts (GET list, POST create), src/app/api/runs/[id]/route.ts (GET detail, DELETE), src/app/api/runs/[id]/rerun/route.ts (POST), src/app/api/runs/[id]/logs/route.ts (GET), src/app/api/runs/[id]/status/route.ts (GET)
Create API routes: src/app/api/prompts/route.ts (GET list), src/app/api/prompts/refresh/route.ts (POST), src/app/api/prompts/[id]/snapshot/route.ts (GET)
Create API routes: src/app/api/dashboards/route.ts (GET list), src/app/api/dashboards/[slug]/route.ts (GET)
Create UI components: src/components/ui/StatusBadge.tsx, RunCard.tsx, VariablesForm.tsx, RunProgress.tsx, LogViewer.tsx, Navigation.tsx
Update src/app/globals.css with dark theme design tokens
Update src/app/layout.tsx with Navigation and dark theme
Replace src/app/page.tsx with runs list page
Create src/app/new/page.tsx - new run form page
Create src/app/runs/[id]/page.tsx - run detail page with progress and logs
acceptance_criteria: All API routes return proper JSON responses with correct HTTP status codes
POST /api/runs creates a DashboardRun and enqueues a BullMQ job
GET /api/runs returns paginated list of runs ordered by createdAt desc
GET /api/runs/[id]/status returns current status and progress
POST /api/runs/[id]/rerun creates a new run with same parameters
POST /api/prompts/refresh triggers prompt reload from Google Docs
GET /api/dashboards returns list of completed dashboards
Dark theme applied consistently with design tokens from briefing
Navigation component present on all pages
verification: Run npm run build and confirm it exits with code 0
findings: Build succeeds (exit code 0). Redis ECONNREFUSED warnings during build are expected since Redis is not running - these are non-blocking and come from BullMQ queue initialization at import time. All API routes and pages compile correctly with Next.js 16 App Router conventions. Used params: Promise<{id: string}> pattern for dynamic route params as required by Next.js 16.
